originalTitle: New outbreak of Ebola kills 65 in eastern DR Congo
title: A Sad Sickness in Our Neighboring Country
content:
Hello, super curious kids! It’s your friendly African news reporter here, ready to share some important news with you. Today, we're going to talk about something happening in a country called the Democratic Republic of Congo. It's a country right here in Africa, just like ours! Sometimes, people there are getting a bit sick, and we want to understand what's happening and how people are helping them.

A Sickness Called Ebola

Imagine tiny, tiny things you can't even see, even if you try really, really hard. We call these tiny things 'germs.' Most germs are harmless, but some can make us feel unwell. There's a special kind of germ that causes a sickness called Ebola. When someone gets Ebola, they might feel very tired, have a headache, or get a fever. It’s a bit like when you have the flu, but it can make people very, very weak. The good news is that doctors and nurses are super brave and work very hard to help people who get sick with Ebola. **Did you know?** Did you know that germs are everywhere? On your toys, on your hands, even in the air! That's why washing our hands often is super important to keep away the bad germs that can make us sick. Clean hands are happy hands!

Where is DR Congo?

The Democratic Republic of Congo, or DR Congo for short, is a really big country in Africa. If you look at a map of Africa, you'll see it right in the middle, like a big, beautiful heart of our continent. It has lots of tall trees in forests, big rivers, and amazing animals like gorillas and okapis. The sickness we are talking about is happening in the eastern part of DR Congo. This means it’s happening in one special area, not all over the whole country, which is a good thing!

Friends Who Are Not Feeling Well

Sadly, in this part of DR Congo, some grown-ups and children have caught the Ebola sickness. Africa’s top health helpers, who are very smart doctors and scientists, have told us that about 246 people have felt unwell because of Ebola. That's like having almost two-and-a-half big classrooms full of people. But don't worry, many of these people are getting help from doctors and nurses who are working day and night to make them feel better and strong again. They are receiving special care.

Sad News About Some Friends

Sometimes, when people get very, very sick with Ebola, their bodies can get too weak to fight the germs. We have heard that about 65 people have sadly passed away because of this sickness. This makes us feel sad for their families and friends. It’s a bit like losing a pet that you love very much, and it makes our hearts feel a little heavy. But remember, the doctors are trying their absolute best to make sure this number doesn't grow and that more people get better and go home. **Did you know?** Did you know that doctors and nurses are like real-life superheroes? They wear special clothes and masks to keep themselves safe from germs, and they work very hard to find cures and help people heal from all kinds of sicknesses, not just Ebola! They are so brave!

How Does This Sickness Spread?

You might be wondering, 'How do people get this sickness?' Well, Ebola germs are tricky. They don't fly through the air like some cold germs. Instead, they can spread when a sick person’s blood or other body fluids touch someone else. This means if you hug someone who is very, very sick with Ebola, or touch their things without washing your hands, you could get the germs too. That's why doctors wear special gloves and masks when they help sick people, to keep themselves safe and stop the germs from spreading to others. It’s all about being very careful.

What Can We Do to Be Safe and Strong?

Even though this sickness is far away for most of us, there are always good habits we can follow to stay healthy and strong every day! * **Wash your hands!** This is super important. Wash them with soap and water for as long as it takes to sing 'Happy Birthday' twice! This washes away any tricky germs from your hands. * **Don't touch your face!** Try not to touch your eyes, nose, and mouth with your hands, especially if you haven't washed them. Germs love to sneak in through these places. * **Eat yummy food!** Eating healthy fruits like bananas and mangoes, and vegetables like spinach, helps your body stay strong and fight off germs. Your body needs good fuel! * **Get lots of sleep!** Sleeping helps your body rest and recharge so it can be super ready to play and learn. A rested body is a strong body! **Did you know?** Did you know that washing your hands properly can remove millions of tiny germs? It’s like having a superpower right at your fingertips! You are like a super clean hero!

How Doctors and Helpers Are Working Hard

In DR Congo, there are many amazing doctors, nurses, and health workers from Africa and all over the world. They are working together like a super team! They are giving medicines to people who are sick to help them feel better. They are also teaching everyone the best ways to stay safe, like washing hands and being careful. There are even special shots, called vaccines, that can help protect people from getting Ebola. These helpers are truly heroes who care deeply about everyone.

Why Do We Talk About This?

We talk about this news because we are all part of the big African family. Even if we live in different countries, we care about our neighbors. When our friends in DR Congo are going through a tough time, we want to know, we want to send them our good wishes, and we want to know that smart people are helping them. It shows that we are strong when we work together and care for each other across our beautiful continent.

A Message of Hope

Remember, even when there’s a tricky sickness, there are always good people working hard to make things better. Doctors and nurses are learning more about Ebola every day, and they are getting better at helping people. We can send our kind thoughts to everyone in DR Congo, hoping they get well soon and stay safe. By being clean, healthy, and kind, we can all help make our continent a wonderful place where everyone can be strong and happy!
summary: Sadly, some friends in the Democratic Republic of Congo are getting sick with Ebola, but brave doctors and helpers are working very hard to make everyone better and keep others safe.
whyMatters: It matters because we are all neighbors in Africa, and when someone in our big family is having a hard time, we care about them and want to know that people are helping them get well and stay safe.

originalTitle: Former Nigerian minister sentenced to 75 years in rare corruption verdict
title: Former Nigerian minister sentenced to 75 years in rare corruption verdict
content: Former Power Minister Saleh Mamman was convicted last week - but the authorities do not know his whereabouts.

originalTitle: 'They shot my neighbour in the head' - the lakeside city traumatised by war
title: 'They shot my neighbour in the head' - the lakeside city traumatised by war
content: Rebel fighters and Rwandan troops are accused of committing atrocities after capturing the DR Congo city of Uvira in December.

originalTitle: 78 years later: Palestinians are still denied their right to return.
title: 78 years later: Palestinians are still denied their right to return.
content: 78 years ago, 800,000 Palestinians were displaced by zionist militias. Today, their grandchildren are still refugees.

originalTitle: Trump-Xi summit: China, US disagree on what they agreed on
title: Trump-Xi summit: China, US disagree on what they agreed on
content: US touted trade deals; China said it warned Washington over Taiwan. Neither side confirmed the other's claims.

title: Philippines vows to hand fugitive senator to ICC following shootout
content: Senator Ronald 'Bato' dela Rosa wanted for alleged role as top enforcer of ex-president Duterte's deadly drug war.

originalTitle: Trump and Xi conclude 'very successful' talks but no deals confirmed
title: Trump and Xi conclude 'very successful' talks but no deals confirmed
content: There were plenty of choreographed ceremonies but no trade breakthroughs after the two-day visit.

title: CIA chief visits Cuba as energy crisis worsens
content: The reported visit to Havana came after the US renewed an offer of aid to ease the effects of its oil blockade.

title: Carrick future to be decided 'pretty soon' amid contract talks
content: Manchester United head coach Michael Carrick says his future will be decided soon amid contract talks.

originalTitle: Hearts ready to 'rip up script' in title showdown
title: Hearts ready to 'rip up script' in title showdown
content: Head coach Derek McInnes has faith that Hearts can "rip up the script" and prevail in a "pure box office" final-day showdown at Celtic Park with the Scottish Premiership title on the line.

originalTitle: Coleman to end 17-year Everton playing career
title: Coleman to end 17-year Everton playing career
content: Seamus Coleman will finish his Everton playing career at the end of the season, when his contract expires after 17 years at the club.
